_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to describe how the structure and language features of stories create particular effects, and to share our opinions about what we read.

_source.w: We are learning to describe how the structure and language features of stories create particular effects, and to share our opinions about what we read.
y: Analysing how texts work helps us move from simply enjoying stories to understanding what makes them effective.
Sharing informed opinions builds our critical literacy.
This analytical skill helps us improve our own writing by learning from others.
eg: We describe how short, punchy sentences create tension in a suspenseful story.
We explain how an author's use of descriptive language makes a setting feel vivid and real.
We share our opinion on a book and support it with references to specific text features.
We discuss how a text's structure — like a flashback or a surprise ending — affects the reader.
We compare two stories and explain which one uses language more effectively.
We write a review that evaluates both the strengths and weaknesses of a text.
